ð° GAYâS CREAMERY, DAWLISH ð°

Celebrating their one hundredth birthday in business this year is the wildly popular Gayâs Creamery in Dawlish. 

Serving a wide variety of cakes, pasties, Cheesy Steves, cream teas and their ever popular ice cream with a big dollop of clotted cream on top! 

To celebrate their centenary, this beautiful vintage Bedford van is sitting outside the shop and it looks magnificent. Have you been to see it yet? 

Dawlish is full of wonderful independent family-run businesses, itâs what makes this town so very special. So make sure you come to Dawlish soon and enjoy the unique shopping experience and a plethora of tasty food! 

Fancy holidaying in Dawlish next year? Take a look at Pilchard Cottage here ð http://www.sykescottages.co.uk/cottage/Devon-Dawlish/Pilchard-Cottage-1143949.html

Follow @entreprneurinsights for more insights like this

Potato Parcel is a quirky and unique service that allows people to send custom messages or images printed on potatoes, making for a humorous and memorable gift.

Founded in 2015, it gained widespread attention after appearing on Shark Tank, where entrepreneur Mark Cuban invested in the business. 

The concept is simple yet entertaining: users choose a potato, add their personalized message or photo, and the company delivers it to the recipient.

Potato Parcel has become a viral sensation, proving that even the most unconventional ideas can find success in niche markets.

Credits: @potatoparcel @usatoday

messages.content: She managed to keep it a secret for about 6 years. She didnât want Quad Dâs or nothing, just to look normal. So she could be happier. Most unusual weekend ever.

About 8 years back, my step sister Ains, invites me out to a road trip to Sydney, because she said she had an important question to ask me, and Iâm likeâ¦ âahhh, what the hell could she possible want from me?â

Her kids had god parents already, maybe she wants a loan, maybe sheâs dying and needs me to take her to a death dealer, I just agreed and turned up, we get to Sydney and she has an appointment with a plastic surgeon specialist and explains to me that she has a genetic abnormality with the old fatty tissue lobes in the front! So I was like âyeah ok, fair, whatâs this got to do with me?!â She didnât trust her family with the admission of âher thereâs a problem Iâm having an operationâ so it was all on me. I became the âbreast carer.â Took her to and from the appointment, which included an overnight stay in a weird B&B Hotel in the middle of fuck knows where in Western Sydney â¦ the bar man looked like Seth Rogan which amused her immensely trying to come down off anaesthesia.
Only 5 Camels!? Old man was cooked,â¦ 8 Camels and a Potato farm!
I need to watch that properly. I was about 4 episodes in and ended up getting work to do and lost track of it.

messages.content: Scary stuff. At least youâre better prepared now. It can be really dangerous passing out with a needle in you

I went for a blood test once and was in the waiting room about an hour, I could hear my heart beat, my entire body lost its liquid, dry throat, I almost passed out in the waiting areaâ¦ by the time I went in, I was so sick. They gave me the test and as she pulled the needle out I felt this rush of something through my whole body, and then I felt my arm go up in the air all on its own, and then nothing. 

Woke up half sitting, half standing, Iâd passed out and had a seizure. They made me sit there and drink some high glucose drink for about 2 hours before I could leave.

OMG, same. Had blood tests every month for the first 15 years of my life. I passed out one day and said âI just canât do this anymoreâ hated them ever since, if the waiting area is full I just go home.
